title: TX mutex held across lora_send() blocks RX callback snapshotting
description:
lora_l2.c:lichen_lora_l2_tx() does NOT hold lora_mutex during lora_send(), which is correct (see comment at lines 479-493). However, lichen_l2.c:lichen_l2_send() holds tx_mutex across the entire lora_send() call via:

  k_mutex_lock(&tx_mutex, K_FOREVER);  // line 289
  ... linearize, compress, build frame ...
  ret = lichen_lora_l2_tx(tx_frame_buf, frame_len);  // line 334
  k_mutex_unlock(&tx_mutex);  // line 340

The lora_send() at SF10/255 bytes takes ~500ms airtime. During this period:
- tx_mutex is held
- lichen_l2_enable(false) at line 440 acquires tx_mutex before cleanup

This means disabling the interface while a TX is in progress blocks for up to 500ms. This is acceptable but should be documented.

What goes wrong: Not a correctness bug, but the lock duration may surprise callers expecting lichen_l2_enable(false) to return promptly. A future caller might add timeout handling that masks the real cause.

No code change needed - add a comment noting the expected hold time.
close_reason: Already fixed. The code has a deliberate two-mutex design: tx_buf_mutex protects the TX buffer during the ~500ms blocking lora_send(), while lora_mutex remains available for RX callback snapshotting. See lora_l2.c lines 59-64 (mutex definitions with explanatory comment), lines 224-227 (RX callback snapshot uses lora_mutex), and lines 548-559 (TX holds only tx_buf_mutex during lora_send).
